title: pediomelum pariense
intro: Pediomelum pariense, also known as locoweed, is a type of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Pediomelum pariense include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and promote wound healing. Pediomelum pariense can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or poultice, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
